1.title: Death of Caliph Umar
1.description: Death of Caliph Umar (r. 13–23 AH) by injuries from the attack of Persian slave Abu Lu'lu'a Firuz. Sunnis carry rallies on 1 Muharram to commemorate Umar.

2.title: Battle of Karbala
2.description: The Battle of Karbala was fought on this day in 61 AH. Husayn and most of his male relatives and his small retinue were slaughtered by the Umayyad army by the end of the day. After the battle, the women and children in Husayn's camp were taken prisoner and marched to the Umayyad capital Damascus in Syria.

3.title: Mourning rituals for Karbala
3.description: Most mourning rituals for Karbala take place during the first ten days of Muharram, culminating on the tenth with processions in major Shia cities.

4.title: Death of Baba Farid
4.description: Baba Farid, a Punjabi Sufi saint, died on this day in 665 AH. His death is celebrated (urs) for six days during Muharram, in Pakpattan, Pakistan.

5.title: Muharram Rebellion
5.description: In what became known as the 1196 AH Muharram Rebellion, on this day Bengali Muslims in Sylhet staged one of the earliest anti-British uprisings in the Indian subcontinent.

6.title: Aqsa set as qibla
6.description: On this day, the Islamic prophet Muhammad set the Aqsa mosque in Jerusalem as the qibla, towards which early Muslims prayed. This was superseded later by the ancient Ka'ba sanctuary in Mecca in connection with verse 2:144 of the Quran.

7.title: Arrival of the people of the elephant
7.description: Arrival of the "people of the elephant" in Mecca, a reference to al-Fil (lit. 'the elephant'), a surah (chapter) in the Quran.

1.title: Prisoners of Karbala enter Yazid's Palace
1.description: Prisoners of Karbalā entered Yazid's Palace in Syria.

2.title: Death of Sakina bint Husayn
2.description: Death of Sakina bint Husayn, youngest daughter of Hussain ibn Ali and a prisoner of Karbalā.

3.title: Martyrdom of Ali al-Ridha
3.description: Martyrdom of Ali al-Ridha according to one tradition.

4.title: Grand Magal pilgrimage at Touba
4.description: Grand Magal pilgrimage is celebrated at Touba, Senegal, commemorating the departure of Cheikh Ahmadou Bamba.

5.title: Urs of Ali Hajveri
5.description: Death Anniversary (urs) of Ali Hajveri is celebrated at Data Darbar, Lahore.

6.title: Arba'een (Chehlum)
6.description: Arba'een or Chehlum (the 40th day after Ashura) is observed.

7.title: Hijrah (Migration to Medina)
7.description: Migration was started (Hijrah) from Mecca to Medina by Muhammad with Abu Bakr.

10.title: Martyrdom of Imam Hasan ibn Ali
10.description: Martyrdom of Imam Hasan ibn 'Alī, grandson of Muhammad.

1.title: Fall of the Emirate of Granada
1.description: The fall of the Emirate of Granada, the final Muslim kingdom of al-Andalus.

2.title: Death of Imam Hassan Al-Askari
2.description: Death of Imam Hassan Al-Askari, Twelver Imām (see: Chup Tazia).

4.title: Mawlid (Sunni)
4.description: Sunni Muslims observe Mawlid in commemoration of Muhammad's birthday.

5.title: Death of Umm Rubab
5.description: Death of Umm Rubab (beloved wife of Imam Hussain).

6.title: Birthday of Imam Ja'far al-Sadiq (Shia)
6.description: Shia celebrate the birthday of the Imām Ja'far al-Sādiq.

7.title: Birth of Umm Kulthum bint Ali
7.description: Birth of Umm Kulthum bint Ali.

8.title: Death of Khwaja Sirajuddin Naqshbandi
8.description: Death of Khwaja Sirajuddin Naqshbandi, a Naqshbandi Sufi shaykh.

9.title: Eid-e-Zahra
9.description: Eid-e-Zahra (a.k.a. Eid e shuja), a celebration of Shi'ah Muslims.

10.title: Marriage of Muhammad and Khadijah
10.description: Marriage of Muhammad to Khadijah bint Khuwaylid.

11.title: Building of the Quba Mosque
11.description: Building of the Quba Mosque (first mosque in Islam).

12.title: Islamic Unity Week
12.description: The week including 12th and 17th is called Islamic Unity Week in Iran to address both Sunni and Shia views on the birth date of Muhammad.

1.title: Birth of Hasan al-Askari
1.description: The birth of the Eleventh Imam Hasan al-Askari.

3.title: Death of Abdul-Qadir Gilani
3.description: Death of Abdul-Qadir Gilani, the Sufi sheikh who is believed to be the 'saint of saints'.

5.title: Death of Ibn Arabi
5.description: Death of Ibn Arabi, the great philosopher from Andalus who died and rests in Damascus, Syria.

1.title: Battle of Mu'ta
1.description: The Battle of Mu'ta took place in the village of the same name (in present-day Jordan), in which the Muslim army (dispatched by Muhammad) fought against the army of the Byzantine Empire and their Ghassanid vassals reportedly in retribution for the murder of a Muslim emissary by a Ghassanid chief, Shurahbil ibn Amr. The battle was won by the Byzantines, and amongst the losses the Muslims incurred were three of the most prominent Companions of Muhammad: Zayd ibn Haritha, Ja'far ibn Abi Talib, and 'Abd Allah ibn Rawaha.

4.title: Death of Fatima
4.description: Fatima, daughter of Muhammad, died in Medina at the young age of 23 years according to Sunni Muslim sources.

5.title: Burial of Fatima by Ali
5.description: Fatima was buried by her husband Ali.

1.title: Death of Fatima
1.description: Death of Muhammad's daughter Fatima in 11 AH.

2.title: Death of Harun al-Rashid
2.description: Death of Harun al-Rashid, the fifth Abbasid caliph.

3.title: Battle of the Camel
3.description: Victory of Ali in the Battle of the Camel.

4.title: Death of Umm al-Banin
4.description: Death of Umm al-Banin (the mother of Abbas ibn Ali).

5.title: Birth of Fatima Zahra
5.description: Birth of Muhammad's daughter Fatima Zahra.

1.title: Battle of Tabouk
1.description: The Battle of Tabouk took place in Rajab, 9 AH.

2.title: Second pledge at al-Aqabah
2.description: The Second pledge at al-Aqabah took place in Rajab.

4.title: Death of Ali al-Naqi and Uwais al-Qarni
4.description: Death of 'Ali al-Naqi (Twelver Imam) and Uwais al-Qarni.

7.title: Anniversary of Khawaja Moinuddin Chishti
7.description: Many Sufi followers of the Chishti tariqa (path) celebrate the anniversary of Khawaja Moinuddin Chishti.

8.title: Festival of Imam Musa al-Kazim
8.description: Twelvers observe the Festival of Imam Musa al-Kazim in dedication of Musa' al-Kadhim. This is so as to avoid missing celebrating the birth of the seventh imam, which took place in Safar.

9.title: Death of Nazim Al-Haqqani
9.description: Death of Nazim Al-Haqqani, a Turkish Cypriot Sufi Muslim sheikh and spiritual leader of the Naqshbandi tariqa.

12.title: Birth of Ali ibn Abi Talib

14.title: Death of Akhundzada Saif-ur-Rahman Mubarak
14.description: Death of Akhundzada Saif-ur-Rahman Mubarak, the founder of the Naqshbandi Mujaddidi Saifia Tariqa.

17.title: Koonday (Tablecloth dinner)
17.description: In India and Pakistan, Koonday (tablecloth dinner) is organized. It is an occasion for Muslims to discuss Allah and the Ahlul Bayt and to strengthen ties among the Sunni Sufi community with love and compassion.

18.title: Death of Mu'awiya
18.description: Death of Mu'awiya, first caliph of the Umayyad Caliphate.

19.title: Death of Musa al-Kadhim
19.description: Death of Musā' al-Kādhim, seventh Twelver Imam.

20.title: Death of Al-Nawawi
20.description: Death of Al-Nawawi, Shafi'ite jurist and hadith scholar.

21.title: Death of Abu Talib
21.description: Death of Abu Talib ibn Abdul Muttalib, uncle of Muhammad and father of Ali.

22.title: Isra and Mi'raj
22.description: Event of Isra and Mi'raj (Muhammad's night journey and ascension).

23.title: Conquest of Jerusalem
23.description: Conquest of Jerusalem by the Ayyubids.

6.title: Death of Fizza
6.description: Death of Fizza, the hand-maiden (Qaneez) of Fatimah.

8.title: Birth of Ali al-Akbar ibn Husayn
8.description: Birth of Ali al-Akbar ibn Husayn.

9.title: Laylat al-Bara'at (Mid-Sha'ban)
9.description: Holiday known as Laylat al-Bara'at or Nisfu Sha'ban; birth of Muhammad al-Mahdi.

12.title: Death of Sayyad Laal Shah Hamdani
12.description: Death of Sayyad Laal Shah Hamdani.

1.title: Beginning of Ramadan
1.description: Beginning of Ramadan, the month of fasting (sawm), communal prayer, reflection, study of the Quran, charity, and strengthening community ties. Muslims worldwide observe this as one of the five pillars of Islam.

2.title: Laylat al-Qadr (Night of Power)
2.description: Laylat al-Qadr or the Night of Power is the night that Muslims believe the Quran was first sent down to the world and Muhammad received his first quranic revelation. It is considered the holiest night of the year and is generally believed to have occurred on an odd-numbered night during the last ten days of Ramadan.

3.title: Assassination of Ali (struck)
3.description: Ali, the fourth Rashidun caliph and the first Shia Imam, was struck during morning prayer on the 19th day of Ramadan, 40 AH.

4.title: Death of Ali
4.description: Ali died on the 21st day of Ramadan, 40 AH. Shi'ites engage in mourning and prayer on these nights, especially in Iran.

5.title: Eid al-Fitr
5.description: Eid al-Fitr marks the end of Ramadan and the beginning of Shawwal. It is declared after a crescent new moon has been sighted or after 30 days of fasting if no sighting of the moon is possible.

1.title: Eid al-Fitr
1.description: Eid al-Fitr is celebrated throughout the Muslim World for the first three days of this month (but celebrations last until the 29th/30th day as per the festive season's duration).

2.title: Battle of Uhud
2.description: Early Muslims took part in the Battle of Uhud.

4.title: Birth of Muhammad al-Bukhari
4.description: Muhammad al-Bukhari, primary traditionist of the Sunni Muslims, was born in 194 AH.

5.title: Urs of Amir Khusro
5.description: Urs of Sufi Mystic and Poet Amir Khusro.

6.title: Death of Haji Dost Muhammad Qandhari
6.description: Death of Haji Dost Muhammad Qandhari, an Afghan Sufi master of Naqshbandi tradition.

1.title: Birth anniversary of Fatima bint Musa
1.description: Birth anniversary of Fatima bint Musa.

3.title: Battle of the Trench
3.description: The Muslims took part in the Battle of the Trench (Khandaq).

5.title: Pledge of the Tree
5.description: Pledge of the Tree took place.

6.title: The first pilgrimage (Umrah)
6.description: The first pilgrimage - the return to Mecca for the performance of Umrah by Muhammad and his companions.

7.title: Hajj made obligatory
7.description: Hajj was made incumbent upon Muslims in 8 AH.

8.title: Birth of Imam Ali al-Rida
8.description: Birth anniversary of Imam Ali ibn Musa al-Rida, the eighth Twelver Imam.

9.title: Martyrdom of Imam Ali al-Rida
9.description: Martyrdom of Imam Ali al-Rida according to one tradition.

10.title: Day the Earth was laid beneath the Ka'ba
10.description: Arabic: يوم دحو الارض, the day earth was laid beneath the Ka'ba, and the birth date of Ibrahim and Jesus.

11.title: Martyrdom of Imam Muhammad al-Jawad
11.description: Martyrdom of Imam Muhammad ibn Ali at-Taqi al-Jawad, the ninth Twelver Imam.

1.title: Marriage of Ali and Fatima (Shi'ite)
1.description: Nikah (marriage) of Ali and Fatima – AH 2.

2.title: Martyrdom of Muhammad al-Baqir (Shi'ite)
2.description: Martyrdom of Twelver and Ismā'īlī Shī'ite Imām, Muhammad al-Bāqir – AH 114.

3.title: Husayn begins journey to Karbala
3.description: Husayn ibn 'Alī began his journey to Karbalā' from Mecca.

4.title: Hajj pilgrimage
4.description: The days of the Hajj pilgrimage take place from the 8th to the 13th of Dhu'l-Hijja.

5.title: Day of Arafah
5.description: The Day of Arafah. Observing a fast on this day purifies the sins of this life. It is also a day of supererogatory fasting.

6.title: Martyrdom of Muslim ibn Aqeel
6.description: Martyrdom of Muslim ibn 'Aqīl and Hani ibn Urwah in Kufah – AH 60.

7.title: Eid al-Adha
7.description: Eid al-Adha (Festival of the Sacrifice) begins on the 10th day and ends on the 13th day. It commemorates the willingness of Ibrahim to sacrifice his son.

8.title: Assassination of Uthman
8.description: Assassination of Uthman, the prominent companion and son-in-law of Muhammad and Khadija.

9.title: Birth of Ali al-Hadi (Shi'ite)
9.description: Birth of Twelver Imām, 'Alī al-Naqī – AH 214 (Disputed date).

10.title: Eid al-Ghadir
10.description: Eid al-Ghadir is celebrated by Shi'ite Muslims, commemorating the event of Ghadir Khumm – AH 10.

11.title: Fatimah moves to Ali's house
11.description: Fatimah went to Ali's house after their marriage.

12.title: Martyrdom of Meesam Tammar
12.description: Martyrdom of Meesam Tammar, friend of Ali – AH 60, and martyrdom of two sons of Muslim ibn 'Aqīl in Kufa – AH 60.

13.title: Event of the Mubahala
13.description: Event of the Mubahala took place ('Eid al-Mubahila). On the same day, the Hadith 'Ahl al-Kisa' event occurred prior to Muhammad setting out for Mubahala, and Ali gave alms with the ring.

14.title: Ali becomes Caliph
14.description: Ali becomes the Caliph of Islam – AH 35.
